title: Delicate Crisp Ukrainian Butternut Squash Pilaf
description: A traditional Ukrainian dish featuring roasted butternut squash and a hint of sweetness from candy, made low-fat-friendly, this pilaf is a briny and crisp nostalgic treat. It combines the warmth of Ukrainian cuisine with the comfort of a hearty pilaf.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Toss the butternut squash with 1 tablespoon of olive oil, salt, and pepper on a baking sheet, and roast for 20 minutes or until tender.
In a large saucepan, heat the remaining 1 tablespoon of olive oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion to the saucepan and cook until it is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic to the saucepan and cook for an additional 1 minute.
Add the chopped candy, chicken broth, and uncooked rice to the saucepan, stirring to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 18 minutes or until the rice is tender and the liquid has been absorbed.
Stir in the roasted butternut squash and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Season with salt and pepper to taste, then garnish with chopped fresh parsley.
